items.title: How to Get Rid of Glitter: A Cleaning Guide for Every Surface
items.summary: Glitter is fun and festive, adding sparkle to arts and crafts, holiday decorations, and even makeup. But when it spills, it can seem impossible to remove, sticking to every surface and lingering for weeks. If you've found yourself battling an unexpected glitter explosion, don't worry—these…
items.content_html:
Glitter is fun and festive, adding sparkle to arts and crafts, holiday decorations, and even makeup. But when it spills, it can seem impossible to remove, sticking to every surface and lingering for weeks. If you've found yourself battling an unexpected glitter explosion, don't worry—these tried-and-true cleaning techniques will help you reclaim your space with minimal effort.

Best Ways to Remove Glitter from Different Surfaces

1. Lint Rollers: Your First Line of Defense

Lint rollers are incredibly effective for picking up glitter from fabric, upholstery, and hard surfaces. Simply roll the adhesive over the glittered area, peeling away layers as needed. This works well for:

  • Sofas and chairs
  • Clothing and fabric
  • Table surfaces

Pro Tip: If you don’t have a lint roller, wrap duct tape or masking tape around your hand, sticky side out, and dab the affected areas.

2. Vacuuming: Essential for Carpets & Floors

If glitter has landed on carpets, rugs, or even hard floors, a vacuum cleaner with a hose attachment is your best bet. For optimal results:

  • Use a handheld vacuum or the hose attachment for precise suction.
  • If on hardwood or tile, follow up with a microfiber cloth to remove any stubborn pieces.
  • For plush carpets, try lightly brushing with a rubber squeegee before vacuuming to lift embedded glitter.

3. Microfiber Cloths for Finishing Touches

A slightly damp microfiber cloth can grab any stray pieces of glitter still clinging to surfaces. Use a light patting motion on furniture, countertops, and even skin.

Caution: Check fabric cleaning instructions before using a damp cloth on upholstered furniture.

4. Play Dough or Sticky Tack for Tiny Spots

For small spaces or hard-to-reach areas, press play dough, sticky tack, or a wad of tape onto the glitter. This technique works especially well on keyboards, cracks in furniture, and tight corners.

5. Swiffer Dry or Wet Cloths for Hard Surfaces

For hardwood, tile, or linoleum floors, a Swiffer dry cloth will attract and trap glitter efficiently. For a more thorough clean, follow up with a damp Swiffer or mop to pick up any remaining specks.

Preventing Future Glitter Catastrophes

Even though cleaning glitter can be a challenge, you can prevent major messes with a few proactive strategies:

  • Designate a Glitter Zone: If you or your kids enjoy glitter crafts, limit usage to a single area, preferably one with easy-to-clean floors.
  • Use Trays or Kraft Paper: Place a large tray or kraft paper under any glitter-related project to contain the spread.
  • Change Clothes After Glitter Use: Glitter sticks to clothing and transfers easily throughout the house. Have kids change clothes immediately after a craft session and wash garments separately.
  • Seal Glitter Projects: If you’re using glitter on artwork or crafts, consider sealing it with a spray adhesive to prevent fallout over time.

Book a Professional Clean for a Glitter-Free Home

If you’re dealing with a particularly stubborn glitter spill or just want your home looking spotless, Maid 4 Cleaning is here to help! Our professional cleaning services ensure every last speck of glitter—and any other mess—is gone. Call us today at (314) 626-5885 to schedule your cleaning.

Be Swept Away with Maid 4 Cleaning!

items.title: How to Clean Your Instant Pot Like a Pro
items.summary: At Maid 4 Cleaning, we love a kitchen gadget that makes life easier—and the Instant Pot does just that. From fast family meals to set-it-and-forget-it soups and stews, it has become a staple in homes across St. Louis. However, while it excels at cooking, it…
items.content_html:
At Maid 4 Cleaning, we love a kitchen gadget that makes life easier—and the Instant Pot does just that. From fast family meals to set-it-and-forget-it soups and stews, it has become a staple in homes across St. Louis. However, while it excels at cooking, it struggles with cleaning itself. That’s where we come in.

Here's your comprehensive, hassle-free guide to cleaning your Instant Pot safely and thoroughly, ensuring it stays fresh, functions properly, and continues to deliver delicious results. Because when it comes to cleanliness, you deserve to Be Swept Away.

Instant Pot Cleaning Basics

Before diving into deep cleaning, it’s essential to understand how to maintain your Instant Pot with regular care. This responsibility ensures the longevity of your Instant Pot. After each use, unplug the device and allow it to cool down. Then follow these easy steps:

What You’ll Need:

  • Mild dish soap

  • Warm water

  • Microfiber cloths and a sponge

  • Small scrub brush or old toothbrush

  • Distilled white vinegar

  • Baking soda (for stuck-on gunk)

  • Shallow pan (for soaking)

  • Silicone-safe degreaser (optional)

Daily Cleaning Steps:

  1. Unplug and Disassemble – Remove the inner stainless steel pot, sealing ring, steam rack, and lid.

  2. Wash Removable Parts – Clean the inner pot, ring, rack, and lid with warm soapy water. These parts are also dishwasher safe, but handwashing helps preserve their condition.

  3. Clean the Lid Thoroughly – Pay attention to the anti-block shield (which can pop off easily) and the float valve. Use a small brush to clean tight areas.

  4. Wipe the Cooker Base – Never submerge the base in water. Instead, use a damp cloth spritzed with a bit of vinegar to wipe it down. Buff dry with a microfiber cloth.

  5. Dry Everything – Let all parts air dry completely before reassembling to prevent mold or mildew growth.

Deep Cleaning Tips (Do This Monthly or as Needed)

Sticky dishes or strong smells (such as chili or curry) require a more thorough cleaning. Here's how to freshen up your Instant Pot:

Step-by-Step Deep Clean:

  1. Soak the Sealing Ring – Submerge it in a shallow pan of vinegar for 5–10 minutes, then scrub and rinse with soapy water. Let it dry completely before reinserting.

  2. Clean the Lid’s Crevices – Use a toothbrush dipped in vinegar to clean around the steam release and float valve.

  3. Descale the Inner Pot – If you notice hard water buildup, fill the pot with a mixture of one part vinegar to one part water. Set the Instant Pot to "Steam" for 2 minutes, then let it cool. Dump the mixture and rinse thoroughly.

  4. Tackle Odors Naturally – Combine one cup of water with a sliced lemon or a few drops of lemon essential oil in the pot. Run on “Steam” for 2 minutes to deodorize.

  5. Buff and Shine – Dry and polish the exterior and all parts with a soft cloth.

Quick Pro Tips from Maid 4 Cleaning

  • Store With the Lid Off – Prevent odor buildup by storing the Instant Pot with the lid upside down.

  • Have Extra Rings – Use separate sealing rings for savory and sweet recipes to prevent flavor cross-contamination.

  • Avoid Harsh Abrasives – Stick with non-scratch sponges and microfiber to protect your cooker’s finish.

Want a Cleaner Kitchen Without the Hassle?

Maid 4 Cleaning offers professional, reliable, and detail-focused home cleaning across Saint Louis. Whether you're juggling a busy schedule or want more time to enjoy your Instant Pot meals, we’re here to help. Let us take the hassle out of maintaining your kitchen, so you can focus on what you love.

items.title: 7 Cleaning Habits of Neat People
items.summary: We all know those people whose homes seem effortlessly clean—always guest-ready and inviting. Their spaces feel like a calm oasis, free from clutter and chaos. What’s their secret? The truth is, maintaining a tidy home isn’t about endless scrubbing but rather about developing smart cleaning…
items.content_html:
We all know those people whose homes seem effortlessly clean—always guest-ready and inviting. Their spaces feel like a calm oasis, free from clutter and chaos. What’s their secret? The truth is, maintaining a tidy home isn’t about endless scrubbing but rather about developing smart cleaning habits.

At Maid 4 Cleaning, we know what keeps homes spotless day after day. Here are seven key habits of neat people that you can adopt today to keep your home in Saint Louis looking pristine.

1. Make the Bed Every Morning

This simple habit instantly transforms your bedroom, setting a positive tone for the rest of the day. Even if you only straighten the comforter and fluff the pillows, your space will feel more organized. Plus, it sets a great example for kids to follow!

Pro tip: If making the bed feels like a chore, simplify by using a duvet cover that’s easy to shake and smooth out in seconds.

2. Stick to a Laundry Routine

Laundry piles up fast, making your home feel cluttered and overwhelming. Tidy people schedule regular laundry days to prevent this.

  • Large households: A load per day keeps things manageable.

  • Smaller households: Choose specific laundry days, such as Wednesday and Sunday.

Bonus tip: Don’t let clean clothes sit in baskets—fold and put them away immediately to maintain order.

3. Do a Weekly Fridge Check

Avoid mystery leftovers and expired items by making fridge cleaning a weekly habit.

  • Toss expired food and forgotten leftovers.

  • Wipe spills immediately to prevent sticky messes.

  • Group similar items together (e.g., dairy, condiments, produce) for easy access.

Saint Louis-specific tip: With our city’s humid summers, be extra mindful of perishable food items that spoil quickly.

4. Clean as You Cook

Cooking can quickly turn your kitchen into a disaster zone. But if you clean as you go, the mess won’t pile up.

Try this routine:

  • Empty the dishwasher before cooking.

  • Fill the sink with warm soapy water to soak used dishes.

  • Wipe spills and crumbs immediately.

  • Use downtime (waiting for water to boil, etc.) to wash prep tools.

After dinner, you’ll only need to rinse a few plates and wipe the counters—no overwhelming pile-up!

5. Declutter Regularly

Tidy homes aren’t just clean—they’re organized. Adopt a decluttering mindset:

  • Follow a one-in, one-out rule for new purchases.

  • Keep a donation box for unwanted clothes and household items.

  • Declutter high-traffic areas like entryways and countertops daily.

Less clutter means less cleaning, making it easier to maintain a neat space.

6. Do a Nightly 10-Minute Tidy-Up

Before winding down for the night, take 10 minutes to restore order:

  • Put away stray items (shoes, mail, toys, etc.).

  • Wipe down kitchen counters and the dining table.

  • Fluff pillows and straighten throws in the living room.

This quick routine prevents messes from piling up, so you wake up to a tidy home.

7. Hire a Professional Cleaning Service

The biggest secret of truly neat people? They don’t do it all alone. A professional cleaning service like Maid 4 Cleaning can take care of deep cleaning tasks, leaving your home consistently spotless with minimal effort.

Why Choose Maid 4 Cleaning in Saint Louis?

  • Expert cleaners trained in proven cleaning techniques.

  • Flexible scheduling—weekly, biweekly, or monthly options.

  • Eco-friendly products that are safe for your family and pets.

Call Us Today: (314) 626-5885

Let us take cleaning off your to-do list so you can enjoy your home stress-free.

Final Thoughts

Clean homes aren’t about spending hours scrubbing—they’re about smart, consistent habits. By making these small changes, you’ll notice a big difference in how effortlessly tidy your home feels.

Need an extra hand? Maid 4 Cleaning is here to help. Contact us today and experience the joy of a professionally cleaned home!

📍 Serving Saint Louis, MO, and surrounding areas.

items.title: 8 Smart Things to Look for When Hiring a Cleaning Service
items.summary: Keeping your home clean shouldn’t be another stressful item on your to-do list. Between work, errands, and kids’ activities, finding time to thoroughly clean can feel nearly impossible. That’s where professional help comes in! But before handing over the keys to just anyone, it’s important…
items.content_html:
Keeping your home clean shouldn’t be another stressful item on your to-do list. Between work, errands, and kids’ activities, finding time to thoroughly clean can feel nearly impossible. That’s where professional help comes in! But before handing over the keys to just anyone, it’s important to know what to look for in a trustworthy, high-quality cleaning company.

At Maid 4 Cleaning, we’ve been proudly serving the Saint Louis area with eco-friendly, professional house cleaning tailored to busy families and individuals. Here's our expert list of what to consider when hiring a maid service near you:

1. Bonded, Licensed, and Insured – What It Really Means

Always ask if the company is licensed, bonded, and insured. A license shows they’re operating legally. Bonding protects you in case of theft or damage. Insurance covers accidents that might happen during the cleaning. A trustworthy cleaning service will gladly provide this documentation.

2. Read Local Reviews

Don’t underestimate the power of a good review. Platforms like Google, Yelp, and Nextdoor offer unfiltered insight into customer experiences. Look for a company with a consistent track record of reliability, friendliness, and professionalism.

“Life savers! I was in a pinch before my son's birthday festivities...had hurt my shoulder and couldn't clean, never used a service before, and had just been canceled by another company. Ella made a next day appointment and came personally to my rescue! She is honest, fair, and professional. Not sure how I'd have pulled it off without her help!! And she made my first experience a breeze! The house was spotless and so fresh....I was humbled by her help!! I will recommend her business to anyone and everyone! Call Ella!!.” — Rene Callahan

3. Know What Type of Cleaning You Need

Not all cleaning is created equal. Are you looking for a weekly spruce-up, a deep seasonal clean, or help with a move-in/out? Whether you want eco-friendly products or need pet-hair removal, make sure the company offers customizable plans. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we tailor every clean to your needs — no cookie-cutter packages here.

4. Budget Realistically

Hiring a cleaning service is an investment in your time and sanity. While it can be tempting to go with the cheapest option, remember: you get what you pay for. Most Saint Louis homeowners find that biweekly or monthly cleanings are surprisingly affordable — especially compared to what they spend on cleaning supplies.

Quick Math:
The average American spends $40–$50/month on cleaning products alone. That’s over $600/year — enough for regular professional cleaning instead!

5. Check the Service List

Does the company offer more than just a wipe-down? Look for deep cleaning options, organization help, green cleaning, and even laundry services. We offer all that and more — because no two homes (or clients!) are the same.

6. Local Matters

National chains may have name recognition, but local companies like Maid 4 Cleaning offer personalized service, consistent cleaners, and a better understanding of your neighborhood’s needs. Plus, we’re just a call away: (314) 626-5885.

7. Ask About Their Cleaning Products

If you're eco-conscious or have allergies, ask what products are used. We focus on safe, effective, non-toxic products and microfiber cloths (which remove up to 99% of bacteria using just water)​.

8. Background-Checked and Trained Staff

Your home is your sanctuary. You deserve to feel safe. Ensure the company does background checks and trains their team thoroughly. At Maid 4 Cleaning, our employees go through vetting, training, and regular quality checks to maintain our high standards.

items.title: How to Clean an Iron Soleplate the Right Way
items.summary: When was the last time you cleaned your iron’s soleplate? If you're like most folks in Saint Louis, the answer might be “never.” But keeping the soleplate clean is essential not only for protecting your clothes from burnt residue and starch build-up but also for…
items.content_html:
When was the last time you cleaned your iron’s soleplate? If you're like most folks in Saint Louis, the answer might be “never.” But keeping the soleplate clean is essential not only for protecting your clothes from burnt residue and starch build-up but also for ensuring your iron glides smoothly and heats efficiently.

At Maid 4 Cleaning, we believe even small maintenance tasks can make a big difference in your home. So here’s your go-to guide on how to properly clean your iron’s soleplate, plus some helpful tips to keep it in top shape.

How to Know It’s Time to Clean Your Iron

If your iron isn’t gliding as smoothly or starts leaving marks on your clothes, it’s probably time for a clean. Heavy use—like daily pressing of uniforms, business attire, or linens—means more frequent cleanings. Even if your iron looks clean, invisible starch and fiber buildup could be affecting its performance.

How to Clean an Iron Soleplate with Baking Soda Paste

You’ll need:

  • Baking soda

  • Distilled water

  • Microfiber cloths

  • Cotton swabs

Steps:

  1. Unplug the iron and let it cool completely.

  2. Mix two tablespoons of baking soda with one tablespoon of distilled water to form a paste.

  3. Gently apply the paste to the iron’s soleplate using a microfiber cloth. Focus on areas with visible gunk.

  4. Wipe clean with a damp cloth to remove all residue.

  5. Use a cotton swab dipped in distilled water to clean the steam holes.

  6. Let the iron dry fully before plugging it in.

This method is simple and safe, especially for irons with nonstick coatings. Always avoid abrasive scrubbers that can scratch the surface.

How to Freshen Up the Soleplate with White Vinegar

You’ll need:

  • Distilled white vinegar

  • A microfiber cloth

Steps:

  1. Heat a small amount of vinegar until warm—never boiling.

  2. Dampen your microfiber cloth with the warm vinegar.

  3. Wipe down the soleplate until it shines.

  4. Follow with a clean damp cloth to remove any lingering vinegar smell.

Note: Always avoid using vinegar inside the iron's water reservoir unless the manufacturer recommends it.

Smart Iron Care Tips from the Pros

To reduce build-up and keep your iron running smoothly, try these pro tips we use in our clients’ homes:

  • Use distilled water only to prevent mineral buildup.

  • Empty the water tank after each use to avoid rust and odors.

  • Wipe down the soleplate with a damp cloth after ironing to remove fresh residue.

  • Avoid ironing over zippers or decals, which can transfer grime to the plate.

If your iron has a self-clean function, use it monthly—or more often if you use it daily.

Final Thought: Treat Your Iron Like a Tool, Not a Toy

A clean iron helps your clothes look crisp and keeps fabrics safe. Whether you’re pressing school uniforms, work shirts, or those lovely pillowcases before guests arrive, a clean iron gets the job done faster—and better.

For more helpful tips, or if you’d rather not worry about things like this, give us a call at (314) 626-5885 and let our team at Maid 4 Cleaning handle the dirty work. Be Swept Away with professional service you can count on!

items.title: Bagged vs. Bagless Vacuum Cleaners: Which One is Right for You?
items.summary: When it comes to keeping your home spotless, choosing the right vacuum cleaner can make all the difference. Whether you have hardwood, tile, carpet, or marble flooring, a vacuum is often the first tool you turn to for efficient cleaning. But with so many options…
items.content_html:
When it comes to keeping your home spotless, choosing the right vacuum cleaner can make all the difference. Whether you have hardwood, tile, carpet, or marble flooring, a vacuum is often the first tool you turn to for efficient cleaning. But with so many options available, one of the biggest decisions you’ll face is whether to go for a bagged or bagless vacuum cleaner.

At Maid 4 Cleaning, we know how important it is to have the right tools for the job. Below, we break down the pros and cons of each type so you can make an informed choice for your home.

Bagged Vacuum Cleaners

These traditional models use replaceable vacuum bags to trap dust, dirt, and allergens effectively.

Pros:

✅ Better for Allergies – The bag acts as a built-in filter, trapping dust and allergens and preventing them from re-entering the air. ✅ Mess-Free Disposal – When you remove the bag, all dirt stays contained, making disposal easy and sanitary. ✅ Larger Capacity – Vacuum bags typically hold more debris, meaning fewer changes and less maintenance. ✅ Less Frequent Filter Cleaning – Many bagged models integrate the filter into the bag itself, reducing the need for regular filter washing or replacement.

Cons:

❌ Ongoing Cost of Bags – Since most bags are disposable, you’ll need to purchase replacements regularly. ❌ Reduced Efficiency When Full – A full bag can decrease suction power, requiring frequent monitoring. ❌ Finding Replacement Bags – If your vacuum model is discontinued, it may become harder to find compatible bags. ❌ Mid-Cleaning Interruptions – If the bag fills up in the middle of a cleaning session, you’ll need to stop and replace it.

Bagless Vacuum Cleaners

These modern vacuums use a dustbin or canister instead of a disposable bag, offering a different approach to floor cleaning.

Pros:

✅ No Need for Replacement Bags – Saves money and reduces waste, making it an eco-friendly choice. ✅ Transparent Dustbin – Many bagless vacuums have clear canisters, so you can easily see when it needs emptying. ✅ Consistent Suction Power – Many models maintain suction without performance loss due to a full bag. ✅ Immediate Reusability – Once emptied, the vacuum is ready for use without the need for new supplies.

Cons:

❌ Messy Disposal – Emptying the dustbin can release dust back into the air, which isn’t ideal for allergy sufferers. ❌ Frequent Filter Maintenance – Filters must be cleaned or replaced periodically to maintain performance. ❌ Smaller Capacity – Dustbins tend to fill up faster than vacuum bags, meaning more frequent emptying. ❌ Potential for Clogging – Some bagless models require extra care to prevent clogging and maintain suction.

Which Vacuum is Best for You?

There’s no universal answer—it all depends on your lifestyle, home environment, and personal preferences:

✅ For Allergy Sufferers: A bagged vacuum is the better choice, as it contains dust and allergens more effectively. ✅ For Budget-Conscious Buyers: A bagless vacuum saves money over time by eliminating the need for replacement bags. ✅ For Heavy-Duty Cleaning: If you vacuum frequently or have pets, a bagged model may be more convenient since it requires less frequent emptying. ✅ For Eco-Friendly Cleaning: A bagless vacuum reduces waste, making it a more sustainable choice.

5 Tips for More Effective Vacuuming

No matter which vacuum you choose, proper technique matters! Follow these expert tips for a cleaner home:

1️⃣ Vacuum in Different Directions – Pass the vacuum over carpets and rugs in multiple directions to remove deeply embedded dirt. 2️⃣ Use the Right Attachments – Utilize brush heads, crevice tools, and upholstery attachments for a thorough clean. 3️⃣ Maintain Your Vacuum – Regularly clean filters, replace bags or empty the dustbin, and check for blockages to keep suction power strong. 4️⃣ Vacuum High-Traffic Areas More Often – Entryways, hallways, and living areas accumulate dirt quickly and should be vacuumed at least twice a week. 5️⃣ Don’t Forget Under Furniture – Use extendable hoses or lift furniture to reach hidden dust and debris.

Need a Professional Touch?

If you’re short on time or just want a deeper clean, Maid 4 Cleaning is here to help! Our expert team provides top-tier cleaning services in St. Louis and surrounding areas, ensuring your home stays fresh and spotless.

📞 Call us today at (314) 626-5885 to book your cleaning service and Be Swept Away by our professional touch!

Whether you choose a bagged or bagless vacuum, the key is to find what works best for your home and cleaning habits. And when you need an extra hand, you know who to call—Maid 4 Cleaning!

items.title: Cleaning Shower Door Tracks: A Step-by-Step Guide
items.summary: You’ve cleaned your shower doors and scrubbed the walls, but what about the shower door tracks? Many homeowners in Saint Louis overlook this crucial part of bathroom maintenance. However, shower door tracks collect soap scum, grime, and even mold. Hard water buildup can also make…
items.content_html:
You’ve cleaned your shower doors and scrubbed the walls, but what about the shower door tracks? Many homeowners in Saint Louis overlook this crucial part of bathroom maintenance. However, shower door tracks collect soap scum, grime, and even mold. Hard water buildup can also make sliding doors difficult to open and close. For a spotless bathroom, it’s essential to clean your shower door tracks as often as the rest of your shower.

Why Cleaning Shower Door Tracks Matters

  • Prevents mold and mildew – Shower tracks are a breeding ground for mold due to moisture retention.

  • Removes hard water buildup – Hard water stains make sliding doors difficult to operate.

  • Enhances bathroom appearance – Clean tracks contribute to a sparkling shower.

  • Prolongs shower door lifespan – Regular maintenance prevents track damage from mineral deposits.

Here’s how you can effectively clean your shower door tracks with simple household items and tools.

Method 1: Cleaning Shower Door Tracks with Vinegar (for Non-Stone Surfaces)

Distilled white vinegar is an excellent natural cleaner that breaks down soap scum and hard water stains. However, if your shower floor or tiles are made of natural stone, avoid vinegar as it can cause permanent damage.

What You’ll Need:

  • ¼ to ½ cup distilled white vinegar

  • Cotton balls or paper towels

  • A spray bottle with distilled water

  • A grout brush or an old toothbrush

  • A microfiber cloth or dry towel

Instructions:

  1. Soak cotton balls or paper towels in white vinegar and place them along the shower door tracks. This ensures the vinegar stays in contact with the grime.

  2. Let it sit for 10–15 minutes to dissolve soap scum and mineral deposits.

  3. Use a grout brush or an old toothbrush to scrub away the loosened residue.

  4. Rinse the tracks with a spray bottle filled with water.

  5. Wipe dry with a microfiber cloth.

  6. Repeat as needed for stubborn buildup.

Alternative for Stone Surfaces: If you have natural stone, use a mix of dish soap and warm water instead of vinegar. Apply the solution, scrub with a brush, rinse, and dry.

Method 2: Deep Clean with Baking Soda and Hydrogen Peroxide

If your shower door tracks have heavy grime buildup, this method provides a more powerful clean.

What You’ll Need:

  • 2 tablespoons baking soda

  • 1 tablespoon hydrogen peroxide

  • A small dish

  • A toothbrush

  • A microfiber cloth

Instructions:

  1. Mix baking soda and hydrogen peroxide in a small dish to create a paste.

  2. Apply the paste to the tracks using a toothbrush, ensuring it reaches all crevices.

  3. Let it sit for 5–10 minutes.

  4. Scrub thoroughly with the toothbrush.

  5. Wipe away residue with a damp microfiber cloth.

  6. Dry completely to prevent mold growth.

Why It Works:

  • Baking soda is a mild abrasive that scrubs away dirt.

  • Hydrogen peroxide disinfects and breaks down stains without damaging surfaces.

Method 3: Cleaning Shower Door Tracks with a Steamer

If you prefer a chemical-free solution, a handheld steam cleaner is an excellent option.

What You’ll Need:

  • A steam cleaner with a narrow nozzle attachment

  • Microfiber cloths

  • A plastic spatula or plastic putty knife

Instructions:

  1. Fill the steamer with water and heat according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

  2. Direct the steam nozzle into the shower door tracks to loosen grime.

  3. Use a plastic spatula wrapped in a microfiber cloth to wipe away debris from hard-to-reach areas.

  4. Wipe the tracks dry with a clean microfiber cloth.

  5. Repeat until the tracks are free of buildup.

Why It Works:

  • Steam breaks down soap scum and kills bacteria naturally.

  • No need for chemicals, making it safe for all surfaces.

How to Maintain Clean Shower Door Tracks

Once you’ve deep cleaned your shower door tracks, follow these simple maintenance tips to keep them in top condition:

  • Wipe down the tracks after every shower – Use a microfiber cloth to prevent water buildup.

  • Use a shower squeegee – Reducing excess water minimizes soap scum.

  • Apply a water repellent spray – Products like Rain-X can help prevent buildup.

  • Perform a deep clean monthly – Preventative cleaning saves time and effort.

Don’t Have Time to Clean? Let Us Help!

Keeping up with home cleaning can be overwhelming, especially with a busy schedule. If your to-do list is piling up, let Maid 4 Cleaning take care of it for you! Our professional Saint Louis house cleaning services ensure every corner of your home, including those tricky shower door tracks, is spotless.

📞 Call us today at (314) 626-5885 or visit our website to book a cleaning appointment.

Let Maid 4 Cleaning handle the dirty work, so you can enjoy a fresh and sparkling home!

items.title: How to Clean Your Water Cooler: A Step-by-Step Guide
items.summary: When was the last time you cleaned your water cooler? If you’re unsure, it might be time. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we know how important it is to keep every corner of your home fresh, even the hidden spots you might not think about every…
items.content_html:
When was the last time you cleaned your water cooler? If you’re unsure, it might be time. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we know how important it is to keep every corner of your home fresh, even the hidden spots you might not think about every day. And yes, that includes your water dispenser.

Moisture, dust, and hands all come into contact with your cooler, making it a perfect breeding ground for bacteria and mold. Fortunately, cleaning and sanitizing your water cooler is easier than you think — and it's something every Saint Louis homeowner should do regularly.

Call Maid 4 Cleaning today at (314) 626-5885 to schedule your full home refresh. Be Swept Away!

How Often Should You Clean a Water Cooler?

Most manufacturers recommend cleaning your cooler every 3 to 6 months. If you live in a humid climate or use it heavily, every 3 months is a safe bet.

Before you begin, always consult your cooler’s manual. Not all dispensers are the same, and you don’t want to damage parts during cleaning.

What You’ll Need

  • Distilled white vinegar

  • Mild dish soap

  • Clean microfiber cloths

  • A bucket

  • A large pitcher or jug

  • Rubber gloves (optional but helpful)

  • Spray bottle (for exterior)

  • Soft brush or sponge

How to Clean the Inside of a Water Cooler

Step 1: Unplug and Drain
Always unplug the unit first. Remove the water jug and use the taps to drain any leftover water into a pitcher.

Step 2: Remove Parts
Take out the baffle (the circular part inside) and any water guard or probe system.

Step 3: Wash Removable Parts
Wash the baffle and water guard in warm water with a drop of mild dish soap. Rinse thoroughly and set them on a clean towel to dry.

Step 4: Clean the Reservoir
Mix 1 cup vinegar with 3 cups hot (not boiling) water. Pour it into the reservoir. Using a microfiber cloth or non-abrasive sponge, gently wipe the inside.

Step 5: Rinse Thoroughly
Drain the vinegar solution into a bucket. Rinse twice with clean water, draining fully each time. This ensures no residue affects the taste of your water.

Step 6: Reassemble and Refill
Once everything’s dry, reassemble the cooler, place a new bottle on top, and plug it back in.

Don’t Forget the Outside

Grime can build up around the taps and drip tray, especially in high-traffic homes.

Step 1: Remove and Wash the Drip Tray and Taps
Clean them in warm, soapy water. Rinse and let air dry.

Step 2: Disinfect the Surface
Use a vinegar-filled spray bottle to lightly mist the exterior and wipe clean with a microfiber cloth.

Important Tip: Avoid vinegar near natural stone countertops or floors. Opt for diluted dish soap instead in those areas.

Sanitizing Your Water Cooler

Sanitizing is deeper than just cleaning. Use a 3:1 solution of water to hydrogen peroxide or a commercial food-safe sanitizer (per your cooler’s manual). Never mix bleach with vinegar.

If you're unsure, ask your water delivery service if they offer sanitization or reach out to Maid 4 Cleaning — we can help make sure your water cooler is spotless and safe.

Final Thoughts

Maintaining your water cooler is one of those small chores that makes a big difference. Whether it’s for peace of mind, health, or simply better-tasting water, a quick clean a few times a year is all it takes.

If you’d rather skip the hassle, leave it to the pros. Maid 4 Cleaning serves homes throughout Saint Louis with expert, reliable service. We’ll handle the details — you enjoy the freshness.

Call us at (314) 626-5885 to book your cleaning. Be Swept Away!

items.title: Decorating Tips That Help You Organize — Not Clutter — Your Home
items.summary: Decorating your home is a fun and personal journey—choosing color palettes, curating artwork, and finding that perfect cozy throw. But here’s a little-known secret: some of those décor choices might actually be making your home look cluttered. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we help Saint Louis…
items.content_html:
Decorating your home is a fun and personal journey—choosing color palettes, curating artwork, and finding that perfect cozy throw. But here’s a little-known secret: some of those décor choices might actually be making your home look cluttered. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we help Saint Louis homes shine—literally and visually. Here’s how to keep your home stylish and serene.

1. Start with a Decluttering Detox

Before you dive into decorating, take a good look around. If you’re displaying five family photo frames on one mantle, ask yourself—do they all need to be there? A less-is-more approach gives your special pieces space to breathe. According to Jessica Allen’s guide on decluttering, the number one cause of visual chaos is simply having too much out on display.

💡 Pro Tip: If it hasn’t been used, admired, or cleaned in a year, donate or store it.

2. Style in Groups, Not Scatters

Randomly spaced objects—one vase here, a candle there—can make a room feel messy. Instead, group similar items by type, size, or material. Nestle candles on a small tray or stack coordinating books beneath a decorative object.

This technique helps the eye rest, not dart around the room.

3. Rethink the Bookshelf

Bookshelves should show off your style, not your storage crisis. Mix vertical and horizontal stacks, include bookends or art, and avoid overpacking. Want to display your whole collection? Color-block them! It creates visual harmony and a touch of personality.

📚 Local Pro Hack: Place books you're less likely to use on higher shelves or in secondary rooms. Let your shelf space breathe.

4. Keep Furniture Fuss-Free

Too many throw pillows, rumpled blankets, or oversized slipcovers can make even the cleanest space feel disheveled. Stick to one or two pillows per seat, and fold throws neatly.

🛎️ Saint Louis Tip: If your furniture faces heavy use (kids, pets, entertaining), choose washable slipcovers and schedule a monthly deep-clean from our team at Maid 4 Cleaning. Call us at (314) 626-5885 for a refresh.

5. Hide the Cord Chaos

We love tech—smart TVs, chargers, speakers—but not the jungle of cords that comes with them. Use cord clips, boxes, or even wrap cords in jute rope for a rustic DIY look.

🔌 Skip taping cords to walls or baseboards—it’s not only unsightly but can be unsafe.

6. Curate, Don’t Clutter, the Fridge

Your kitchen may sparkle, but if your fridge is plastered in magnets and expired coupons, that’s what people notice. Limit what’s on display and rotate kids' art weekly. Bonus: decluttering the fridge’s interior helps reduce food waste!

🧲 Pro Organizer Tip: Store coupons and menus in a magnetic file or dedicated binder.

7. Let Natural Light and Air Flow In

Even the best décor can’t outshine the impact of natural light and fresh air. Keep windows clean, blinds dusted, and use light or sheer curtains to maximize brightness. Light visually expands a room and makes it feel cleaner and calmer.

🌿 Bonus: Indoor plants are a chic way to decorate while naturally improving air quality.

Keep Your Home Clutter-Free and Fabulous

Decorating should complement—not compete with—your home's cleanliness and organization. Want more time to style your space instead of scrubbing it? Let Maid 4 Cleaning take housework off your plate. We’re local, reliable, and ready to help Saint Louis homes shine.

📞 Call (314) 626-5885 to book a cleaning.
✨ Be Swept Away by a home that’s both stylish and spotless.

items.title: 8 Ways to Make Your Dorm Room Look and Feel Bigger
items.summary: College dorm rooms are famously tiny—but they don't have to feel that way. With a few smart design tricks, you can create a space that feels more open, functional, and even a little luxurious. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we believe your space should reflect your…
items.content_html:
College dorm rooms are famously tiny—but they don't have to feel that way. With a few smart design tricks, you can create a space that feels more open, functional, and even a little luxurious. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we believe your space should reflect your style and help you thrive—whether you're studying for finals or just trying to relax after a long day.

We're here to help students and parents alike, from deep cleanings between semesters to decluttering and organization services. Call us at (314) 626-5885 to learn more or request a free quote.

Why Dorm Room Comfort Matters

Your living space plays a big role in your mental health and academic performance. When your dorm feels clean, organized, and cozy, it’s easier to stay focused and relaxed. That’s why it’s worth investing a little effort upfront to set your room up for success—and comfort.

1. Think Vertical: Use Your Wall Space Wisely

Go up, not out. Vertical storage is your secret weapon for saving floor space. Use:

  • Over-the-door organizers for shoes, snacks, or toiletries

  • Floating shelves for books and décor

  • Hooks or Command strips for bags, headphones, and accessories

Choose clear bins so you can see what’s inside, or label containers to stay organized.

2. Choose Furniture That Does Double Duty

Multifunctional pieces are perfect for small spaces. Look for:

  • Lofted or raised beds with storage underneath

  • Storage ottomans for seating and extra space

  • Foldable desks or chairs that tuck away easily

If your dorm allows it, consider a futon for seating during the day and extra sleeping space when guests visit.

3. Use Light Colors to Open Up the Space

Light and neutral tones reflect light and create a sense of airiness. Stick with whites, creams, and soft grays for bedding, rugs, and curtains. Then add pops of color with your pillows, posters, or a fun throw blanket.

4. Let the Light In—And Bounce It Around

Natural light is one of the best tools for making a room feel larger. Avoid heavy curtains and opt for sheer or light-filtering panels instead. Place a mirror across from your window to reflect light and give the illusion of more space.

5. Declutter on a Weekly Basis

Clutter shrinks your space—physically and mentally. Set a 10-minute timer once a week and tidy up:

  • Return items to their proper place

  • Recycle or toss papers and packaging

  • Reassess what you really use (and take home what you don’t)

Pro tip from Maid 4 Cleaning's decluttering guide: Start with visible surfaces first—it gives you a quick win that motivates deeper organizing​.

6. Create "Zones" Within the Room

Dividing your space by purpose can make it feel bigger and more functional:

  • Use a small rug to define your "chill" zone

  • Place your desk near a window for natural light and energy

  • Keep your bed area minimal to maintain calmness

This setup not only makes the room look more intentional but also helps your brain switch gears from work to rest.

7. Keep Decor Simple but Personalized

Too much visual clutter can make a small space feel chaotic. Pick 3–5 pieces that speak to you: framed photos, posters, or a pinboard for notes and mementos. Keep it cohesive with matching frames or a color theme.

Avoid overdoing it with string lights or random knickknacks—they can quickly make the space feel cramped.

8. Use Smart Storage Solutions

Dorms often lack drawer space. Here’s how to cheat the system:

  • Rolling carts fit under desks or next to beds

  • Under-bed bins maximize hidden space

  • Bedside caddies keep essentials within reach

  • Vacuum storage bags are perfect for seasonal clothing

As Maid 4 Cleaning notes, your home (or dorm!) should make you feel calm, not overwhelmed​. Systems that work with your habits will help keep it that way.

Bonus Tip:

For tough messes, try proven solutions like dish soap, hydrogen peroxide, or a dedicated degreaser instead.

Need Help Creating a Clean, Organized Dorm Room?

We’re here to help you love your space. Whether it’s a one-time deep clean, help with decluttering, or packing/unpacking before or after a move, our friendly pros at Maid 4 Cleaning are just a call away.

📍 Serving Saint Louis and nearby colleges
📞 Call us today at (314) 626-5885
✨ Be Swept Away with Saint Louis’ trusted maid service

items.title: Bed and Mattress Cleaning: The Essentials for a Healthier Sleep
items.summary: You might change your sheets regularly, but when was the last time you cleaned your mattress and bedframe? If you’re like most people in St. Louis, it may have been a while—or never. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we’re here to make sure you sleep clean…
items.content_html:
You might change your sheets regularly, but when was the last time you cleaned your mattress and bedframe? If you’re like most people in St. Louis, it may have been a while—or never. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we’re here to make sure you sleep clean and well. A quick freshen-up every few months can make a world of difference to your health and your home’s hygiene.

Why Should You Clean Your Mattress?

Your mattress collects more than just dust—it absorbs dead skin cells, body oils, pet dander, hair, and crumbs. According to cleaning experts, these particles create a cozy environment for dust mites, which can worsen allergies and interrupt your sleep. Regular mattress cleaning helps reduce allergens, eliminate odors, and extend the life of your mattress.

How Often Should You Clean It?

We recommend cleaning your mattress two to four times a year, or more often if you have allergies, pets, or tend to snack in bed.

How to Clean a Mattress: St. Louis-Friendly Tips

Here’s a simple process you can follow at home—or call us at (314) 626-5885 and be swept away by a professional clean.

1. Strip and Launder

Wash your sheets, pillowcases, and mattress protector in hot water to kill bacteria and dust mites.

2. Vacuum Thoroughly

Use the upholstery attachment on your vacuum to clean the surface, seams, and sides of your mattress. Focus on corners and crevices where dust settles.

3. Spot Clean Stains

Use a mild upholstery cleaner or enzyme spray (ideal for organic stains like sweat or urine). Blot the area with a clean damp cloth—never soak the mattress.

4. Deodorize Naturally

Sprinkle baking soda generously over the surface. Let it sit for several hours (or overnight) to absorb odors. Then vacuum again.

5. Flip or Rotate

If your mattress isn’t one-sided, flip it every six months. Otherwise, rotate it top-to-bottom to ensure even wear.

Extra tip: Using a breathable mattress protector helps reduce buildup between cleanings and keeps your mattress fresher longer.

Cleaning Your Bedframe

Don't forget the frame. Wooden, metal, or upholstered bedframes collect dust and grime just like any other furniture.

  • Dust first with a microfiber cloth.

  • For wood or metal, use warm water and mild dish soap.

  • For upholstered frames, vacuum and spot clean with an appropriate fabric cleaner.

Use a soft-bristled brush or cloth for detailed carvings and rinse the cloth often to avoid spreading dirt.

Prefer Professional Help?

At Maid 4 Cleaning, we offer mattress vacuuming and bedframe detailing with our residential cleaning services. If you’re in the St. Louis area and want your bed cleaned without lifting a finger, let our expert team do it for you.

Give us a call at (314) 626-5885 to schedule your next cleaning. Your mattress—and your sinuses—will thank you.

Maid 4 Cleaning – Be Swept Away.

items.title: Cleaning Up After Mud, Rain, and Messy Weather
items.summary: April showers may bring May flowers, but they also leave behind muddy footprints, wet paw prints, and water stains that can make your home feel less than fresh. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we know Saint Louis weather doesn’t always cooperate with your clean home goals.
items.content_html:
April showers may bring May flowers, but they also leave behind muddy footprints, wet paw prints, and water stains that can make your home feel less than fresh. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we know Saint Louis weather doesn’t always cooperate with your clean home goals. That’s why we’ve put together expert cleaning tips to handle mud, water stains, and rainy-day messes like a pro.

Be Swept Away — and let us handle the rest.

How to Remove Water Stains from Wood Floors

Got a wet umbrella or muddy shoes that sat a little too long on your hardwood? Don’t worry—there’s still hope.

Try this method:

  1. Apply Mineral Oil: Use a soft cloth to gently rub mineral oil into the white water stain. Let it sit for several hours or overnight.

  2. Use Mineral Spirits (if needed): If the stain remains, carefully apply mineral spirits using gloves and a soft cloth. Ventilate the area well.

  3. Finish with Furniture Polish: For persistent marks, apply furniture polish or wax in a tone that matches your floor.

🪵 Pro Tip: For deep or dark stains, you may need professional wood floor restoration. Don’t DIY your way into more damage!

How to Clean Mud from Carpet Like a Pro

Mud is more than just dirt—it’s loaded with organic matter and moisture that can make it harder to lift from fibers. Here’s the professional approach:

  1. Let Mud Dry Completely. Trying to clean it while wet pushes it deeper.

  2. Vacuum Thoroughly. Use slow passes to lift dry soil without grinding it in.

  3. Mix Cleaning Solution: Combine 1 teaspoon of mild dish detergent with 1 cup of cool (not hot) water.

  4. Blot, Don’t Scrub: Dab the solution into the stain using a white cloth, then blot with clean water to rinse. Repeat until the stain is gone.

  5. Dry the Area: Use a towel to blot dry, then place a fan nearby if needed.

🐾 Pet Paw Bonus: The same technique works for muddy upholstery and pet messes. Just be sure not to over-wet, as moisture can lead to mildew.

How to Remove Mud Stains from Clothing

Rainy day laundry can be extra frustrating—but there’s a smarter way to lift stains:

  1. Scrape Off Dried Mud: Use the dull side of a butter knife.

  2. Rinse with Cold Water: From the back side of the fabric if possible.

  3. Use an Enzyme Detergent: These are especially effective on organic stains like mud, grass, and sweat.

  4. Check Before You Dry: Heat will set the stain. If needed, repeat using an oxygen-based cleaner before drying.

👕 Stain Tip: Avoid bleach unless you're working with all-white items—it can yellow or damage fabrics if misused.

Bonus Tips from the Experts

Inspired by insights from The Science of Cleaning and A Dirty Guide to a Clean Home:

  • Use Microfiber Cloths: These lift soil and bacteria better than cotton, using less product and water.

  • Distilled Water Cleans Better: Especially when paired with microfiber. No streaks, no minerals.

  • Lemons and Salt can tackle light water stains on glass or stainless steel in a pinch.

Want a Mud-Free Home Without the Hassle?

Don’t stress every time your kids or pets run in from the rain. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we specialize in seasonal deep cleans and routine maintenance to keep your Saint Louis home clean and welcoming—rain or shine.

📞 Call us at (314) 626-5885 or book your service online today!

items.title: Save Time Vacuuming: Smart Strategies
items.summary: At Maid 4 Cleaning in Saint Louis, we know your time is precious. Vacuuming may seem like a simple chore, but if you're not doing it strategically, you could be spending way more time than you need to. With a few pro tips and a…
items.content_html:
At Maid 4 Cleaning in Saint Louis, we know your time is precious. Vacuuming may seem like a simple chore, but if you're not doing it strategically, you could be spending way more time than you need to. With a few pro tips and a smarter approach, you can keep your home fresh and inviting—without sacrificing your whole Saturday.

Let’s dive into how to vacuum efficiently and effectively, so you can get back to enjoying your life (or just binge-watching your favorite show, guilt-free).

1. Start with a Plan

The key to fast, effective vacuuming is planning. Before you even plug in the vacuum, know which rooms you're tackling and in what order. Always dust before vacuuming—Saint Louis pollen and dust can settle fast, and cleaning surfaces first keeps your floors from needing a second pass. Start from the farthest point of the room and work your way out toward the exit.

2. Prep Your Vacuum First

Your vacuum needs regular maintenance to work at its best. According to cleaning experts like Martha Stewart, airflow is everything. A clogged filter, full canister, or tangled brush roller can reduce suction and waste your time. Before each use:

  • Empty the dustbin or change the bag

  • Clean or replace filters (HEPA filters are great for allergy-prone homes)

  • Check for hair or debris in the brush roll

3. Clear the Floor

Take a few minutes to tidy up before vacuuming. Pick up toys, shoes, cables, and any small objects that might block your path or damage the vacuum. Not only will this save time, but it also protects your vacuum from costly repairs.

4. Vacuum Like a Pro

Technique matters. For carpet, make slow overlapping passes to let the vacuum do the work. Push forward slowly and pull back even slower—that’s when most of the dirt is lifted. For hard floors, switch to the right setting or attachment to avoid scattering debris. According to A Dirty Guide to a Clean Home, changing directions occasionally also helps lift embedded dirt more effectively.

5. Vacuum Regularly

The more often you vacuum, the less work it takes each time. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we recommend vacuuming high-traffic areas at least twice a week. This prevents buildup and reduces allergens, especially during Saint Louis’s allergy-heavy spring and fall seasons.

Bonus Tips from the Pros

  • Use microfiber dusters before vacuuming—these trap dust better than traditional cloths.

  • Vacuum in grids for larger rooms—divide the room into zones so you don’t miss spots.

  • Use crevice tools weekly in corners and baseboards where dust hides.

  • Declutter your floors often—clear space makes every clean quicker and more thorough.

Let Us Handle the Heavy Lifting

Too busy to keep up with regular vacuuming? We’ve got you. Maid 4 Cleaning offers flexible home cleaning services across the Saint Louis area. Whether you need weekly tidying or deep seasonal cleaning, our team is here to help you reclaim your time and your peace of mind.

Call (314) 626-5885 today to schedule your cleaning. Be Swept Away.

items.title: 3 Surprising Cleaning Hacks Using Toothpaste
items.summary: At Maid 4 Cleaning in Saint Louis, we’re all about transforming your home into a space that sparkles — and sometimes, the secret isn’t in the cleaning aisle, it’s in your bathroom cabinet. Believe it or not, plain white toothpaste (not gel) can be a…
items.content_html:
At Maid 4 Cleaning in Saint Louis, we’re all about transforming your home into a space that sparkles — and sometimes, the secret isn’t in the cleaning aisle, it’s in your bathroom cabinet. Believe it or not, plain white toothpaste (not gel) can be a secret weapon for tackling tough cleaning jobs around your home. Here are three genius ways to clean with toothpaste that might just sweep you off your feet.

1. Bring Back the Shine: Clean Silver with Toothpaste

Tired of tarnished silverware or dull jewelry? Toothpaste can give your silver a second life — just be cautious and avoid using it on silver-plated items or delicate heirlooms.

Here’s how to do it:

  • Apply a pea-sized amount of white toothpaste to a dry microfiber cloth.

  • Rub gently over your silver item in small circles.

  • Use a soft toothbrush for crevices or intricate patterns.

  • Rinse with warm water and dry with a clean cloth.

Martha Stewart recommends using mild abrasives like baking soda pastes for silver too, but toothpaste offers a convenient alternative when you’re in a pinch​.

2. Make Sneakers Look New Again: Clean Shoes with Toothpaste

Scuffed rubber soles or stained canvas shoes? White toothpaste can help restore their fresh-out-of-the-box look.

For rubber soles:

  • Dab toothpaste on a soft toothbrush.

  • Gently scrub scuffed areas using circular motions.

  • Wipe clean with a damp microfiber cloth.

For canvas shoes:

  • Lightly dampen the area you want to clean.

  • Apply toothpaste with a clean cloth and work it in for 1-3 minutes.

  • Wipe away residue with a damp cloth and let air dry.

Avoid using toothpaste on leather or suede. For delicate materials, follow the manufacturer's care guide.

3. Erase Crayon Marks from Walls

If your little artists got creative on the walls, don’t stress — toothpaste to the rescue.

What you’ll need:

  • A soft toothbrush

  • A small amount of white toothpaste

  • A microfiber cloth

Instructions:

  • Spot test a hidden area first.

  • Gently scrub crayon marks with the toothbrush and toothpaste.

  • Wipe clean with a damp cloth to remove any leftover residue.

For painted walls, Ann Russell (the “TikTok Auntie”) suggests using mild, non-gel toothpaste to lift marks without damaging finishes​.

Bonus Tip: Shine Faucets and Remove Odors

You can also use toothpaste to polish chrome fixtures and neutralize funky smells. Rub a dab on faucets to remove water spots and buff to a shine. And if you’ve got lingering odors on hands after chopping garlic or onions, a quick scrub with toothpaste does wonders.

Ready to Be Swept Away?

At Maid 4 Cleaning, we take pride in knowing the best cleaning tips — and using them to give your home the expert care it deserves. If you're in the Saint Louis area and ready to delegate your deep cleaning, we're here to help.

Call (314) 626-5885 to schedule your cleaning today — and be swept away!

items.title: How Often Should You Wash Your Bedsheets?
items.summary: Let’s talk about your bedsheets—those cozy covers you snuggle into every night. But here’s the truth: if you’re not washing them often enough, you might be cuddling up to more than just soft cotton. At Maid 4 Cleaning here in Saint Louis, we know a…
items.content_html:
Let’s talk about your bedsheets—those cozy covers you snuggle into every night. But here’s the truth: if you’re not washing them often enough, you might be cuddling up to more than just soft cotton.

At Maid 4 Cleaning here in Saint Louis, we know a thing or two about creating a healthy home environment. Whether we’re deep-cleaning your home or just helping you stay on top of chores, fresh, clean bedding plays a big role in your well-being.

So, How Often Should You Wash Your Sheets?

While many people go two weeks between washes, weekly laundering is ideal. According to experts (including insights from Martha Stewart’s Homekeeping Handbook and Ann Russell’s practical cleaning advice), once a week is the sweet spot for most households.

Your sheets accumulate:

  • Sweat and body oils

  • Dead skin cells (yum!)

  • Dust mites and their droppings

  • Allergens like pollen or pet dander

  • Bacteria and fungi

If you or your loved ones suffer from allergies, asthma, skin conditions, or simply love a midnight snack in bed, that’s even more reason to wash weekly.

When Should You Wash More Frequently?

You might need to wash your sheets more often if:

  • You sweat heavily at night

  • You sleep with pets

  • You don’t shower before bed

  • You’re sick or have a skin infection

  • You snack in bed (guilty!)

  • It’s allergy season in Saint Louis

Best Way to Wash Your Sheets

Hot water is your friend—when the fabric allows. Cotton sheets do great in warm or hot water, which kills dust mites and dissolves oils effectively. Always check the care label before washing. Use a gentle detergent and skip fabric softeners, which can coat fibers and reduce breathability.

Eco-tip from “Natural Cleaning Secrets”: Add 1/4 cup of baking soda in the wash and 1/2 cup of white vinegar during the rinse cycle to soften sheets naturally—just never mix vinegar and bleach!

Tips to Keep Sheets Cleaner, Longer

Can’t wash weekly? Life happens. Here are some pro tips from cleaning experts like Dana K. White and Melissa Dilkes Pateras:

  • Shower before bed

  • Avoid oily lotions right before sleep

  • Skip late-night snacking in bed

  • Keep pets off the bed (or use a washable pet blanket)

  • Clean feet or wear socks to bed

  • Use pillow protectors and mattress covers

These habits will help extend the freshness between washes and keep your bedding looking newer longer.

What About Changing Your Sheets?

Changing sheets goes hand-in-hand with washing. Swap them out weekly—especially if you rotate sets. Keep at least two sets per bed, so you're never stuck without a clean one.

If you notice a musty smell, or see visible stains or hair, it’s time to toss them in the wash.

Need Help? Let Us Handle It.

If fresh bedding sounds like a dream you don’t have time for, we’ve got you. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we take pride in the little details—like fresh, sanitized sheets and a spotless home you can truly relax in.

📍Based right here in Saint Louis, we’re your go-to maid service for a home so clean, you’ll be Swept Away.

📞 Call us today at (314) 626-5885 to book your next cleaning!

items.title: How to Clean Microfiber Cloths Properly for Maximum Longevity
items.summary: Microfiber cloths are among the best cleaning tools for dusting, absorbing spills, and removing grime. Their ultra-fine fibers make them incredibly effective at trapping dirt without the need for harsh chemicals. However, to keep these cloths working at peak performance, regular and proper cleaning is…
items.content_html:
Microfiber cloths are among the best cleaning tools for dusting, absorbing spills, and removing grime. Their ultra-fine fibers make them incredibly effective at trapping dirt without the need for harsh chemicals. However, to keep these cloths working at peak performance, regular and proper cleaning is essential. If you’re not washing your microfiber correctly, you might be spreading dirt around instead of cleaning it up!

Here’s how to properly clean microfiber cloths so they last longer and maintain their effectiveness.

Best Ways to Clean Microfiber Cloths

1. Washing Machine Method

One of the easiest ways to clean microfiber cloths is by using your washing machine. Follow these steps to ensure they come out fresh and ready for your next cleaning task:

  • Use warm water (never hot) and select a gentle cycle.

  • Use a mild, liquid detergent—avoid powdered detergent as it can clog the fibers.

  • Skip the fabric softener and bleach—these can break down the microfiber and reduce its effectiveness.

  • Wash only with other microfiber cloths—lint from cotton and other fabrics can stick to the cloths and make them less effective.

  • Dry on low heat or air dry—high heat can damage the synthetic fibers.

✅ Pro Tip: If your microfiber cloths have picked up grease, add a small amount of white vinegar to the wash for extra degreasing power.

2. Hand-Washing Method

For lightly soiled microfiber cloths or if you prefer a manual approach:

  • Fill a sink or basin with warm water and add a few drops of mild dish soap.

  • Gently agitate and soak the cloths for 10–15 minutes.

  • Rinse thoroughly to remove all soap residue.

  • Wring out excess water and lay flat or hang to dry.

✅ Pro Tip: Use a soft-bristle brush to loosen stubborn dirt and grime from the fibers.

3. The Dishwasher Hack

Yes, you can clean microfiber cloths in the dishwasher! This method is ideal for a quick refresh:

  • Lay the microfiber cloth flat on the top rack of your dishwasher.

  • Run a regular wash cycle without detergent.

  • Remove the cloth immediately after the cycle ends and hang it to air dry.

✅ Pro Tip: This method works well for kitchen microfiber cloths that may have grease buildup.

4. The Microwave Sanitization Trick

For a fast refresh and to kill bacteria:

  • Thoroughly rinse the cloth with water so it’s damp.

  • Place the damp cloth in the microwave and heat on high for 60 seconds.

  • Let the cloth cool before removing it.

🚨 Warning: Never microwave a dry microfiber cloth—it can catch fire!

How to Remove Debris from Microfiber Cloths

Even after washing, microfiber cloths may still have lint, hair, or small debris clinging to them. Here are some effective ways to remove these particles:

  • Lint Roller: Roll over the cloth to pick up stuck-on lint, hair, and dust.

  • Brush or Comb: Use a fine-toothed comb or even a lice comb to dislodge debris from the fibers.

  • Tweezers: For tiny stubborn bits, tweezers can be a precise tool to pluck them out.

  • Masking Tape: Wrap tape around your fingers (sticky side out) and dab across the cloth.

  • Vacuum Hose: Lay the microfiber flat and run a vacuum hose over it while keeping the cloth securely in place.

✅ Pro Tip: Shake microfiber cloths outside before washing to reduce the amount of trapped debris.

How Long Do Microfiber Cloths Last?

One of the biggest advantages of microfiber cloths is their durability. A high-quality microfiber cloth can withstand up to 500 washes—which translates to two years or more in a typical household. However, you’ll know it’s time to replace your cloth when it no longer picks up dust or absorbs liquids like it used to.

✅ Pro Tip: Keep separate cloths for different cleaning tasks (e.g., one for glass, one for dusting, one for kitchen spills) to extend their lifespan and avoid cross-contamination.

Final Thoughts

Cleaning microfiber cloths properly ensures they remain highly absorbent and effective for years to come. Whether you opt for the washing machine, hand-washing, dishwasher, or microwave method, following these best practices will keep your cloths in top condition. With proper care, you can enjoy streak-free, chemical-free cleaning and get the most out of your investment!

For a sparkling clean home without the hassle, let Maid 4 Cleaning handle the job for you! Call us at (314) 626-5885 to book a professional cleaning service today.

items.title: How to Clean Painted Walls Without Damaging the Finish
items.summary: Cleaning painted walls can refresh the look of your home and remove dirt, grease, and stains. However, the type and quality of paint greatly impact the cleaning method. Here’s a step-by-step guide to safely clean different types of painted walls while preserving their finish. Paint…
items.content_html:
Cleaning painted walls can refresh the look of your home and remove dirt, grease, and stains. However, the type and quality of paint greatly impact the cleaning method. Here’s a step-by-step guide to safely clean different types of painted walls while preserving their finish.

Understanding Paint Finishes

Paint finishes determine how easy it is to clean a wall. The four main types include:

  • High-Gloss & Semi-Gloss (Baked Enamel, Epoxy Enamel, Automotive Paints) – Durable, stain-resistant, and can withstand scrubbing with mild abrasives and heavy-duty cleaners.

  • Satin & Eggshell (General-Purpose Enamels) – Found in kitchens and bathrooms, these finishes handle moderate scrubbing but should avoid abrasives.

  • Matte & Flat (Latex Flat Paints) – Less washable, requiring gentle cleaning methods.

  • Exterior Paints – Designed for outdoor durability but should only be cleaned with mild detergent and rinsed thoroughly.

Supplies You’ll Need

Gather these supplies before starting:

  • Soft microfiber cloth or sponge

  • Mild dish soap or a gentle all-purpose cleaner

  • Warm water

  • Baking soda (for tougher stains)

  • White vinegar (for grease removal)

  • Soft-bristle brush (for textured walls)

  • Magic eraser (use with caution on matte finishes)

Step-by-Step Guide to Cleaning Painted Walls

1. Dust Before You Wash

Before applying any liquid cleaner, remove dust and cobwebs using a microfiber cloth, duster, or vacuum with a brush attachment. This prevents dirt from turning into a muddy mess when mixed with water.

2. Prepare a Mild Cleaning Solution

  • Mix a few drops of dish soap in a bucket of warm water.

  • For grease-prone areas (kitchens), add a small amount of white vinegar.

  • For spot stains, create a paste with baking soda and water.

3. Test in an Inconspicuous Area

Always test your cleaning solution on a hidden section of the wall to check for paint fading or damage.

4. Clean the Wall in Sections

  • Dip the sponge or cloth into the solution and wring out excess water to prevent over-wetting.

  • Gently wipe the wall in a circular motion.

  • For tougher stains, apply a baking soda paste for a few minutes before wiping.

5. Rinse & Dry the Wall

  • Use a clean, damp cloth to wipe away any soap residue.

  • Dry the wall immediately with a soft towel to prevent streaking.

Special Cleaning Tips by Paint Type

For High-Gloss & Semi-Gloss Walls

  • Use a damp microfiber cloth with mild soap.

  • Avoid abrasive scrubbers that can dull the finish.

For Satin & Eggshell Paint

  • Use a damp sponge with diluted dish soap.

  • Wipe gently to avoid scrubbing off the paint.

For Matte & Flat Paint

  • Use a dry sponge or eraser for light marks.

  • For tougher stains, a water-dampened microfiber cloth with a tiny amount of dish soap works best.

For Exterior Walls

  • Rinse with a garden hose before washing.

  • Use a long-handled brush for stubborn spots.

  • Avoid high-pressure washers unless the paint is in excellent condition.

Pro Tip: Preventing Future Wall Stains

  • Use high-quality washable paint for easier maintenance.

  • Wipe spills immediately to prevent stains from setting.

  • Apply a protective wall finish in high-traffic areas.

Need a Deeper Clean?

items.title: 8 Surprising Household Uses for Witch Hazel
items.summary: At Maid 4 Cleaning, we believe a clean home starts with a few great staples—and witch hazel is one of those underrated gems hiding in your medicine cabinet. Known for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, it’s a skincare favorite, but did you know it’s also…
items.content_html:
At Maid 4 Cleaning, we believe a clean home starts with a few great staples—and witch hazel is one of those underrated gems hiding in your medicine cabinet. Known for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, it’s a skincare favorite, but did you know it’s also a handy helper around the house?

Whether you live in a cozy apartment in the Central West End or a family home in Webster Groves, these clever uses for witch hazel can freshen up your space—naturally.

A Quick Note First

Witch hazel is not a disinfectant. While it offers skin-soothing benefits, it won’t eliminate viruses or bacteria like bleach, hydrogen peroxide, or isopropyl alcohol. For true disinfection, stick with EPA-approved cleaners—or leave it to the pros. Maid 4 Cleaning proudly offers enhanced disinfection services using hospital-grade products. Call us at (314) 626-5885 to schedule yours.

1. Wipe Down Window Blinds with Ease

Blinds collect dust, grime, and in the kitchen—grease. Witch hazel can cut through buildup gently without damaging materials.

DIY Blind Cleaner:

  • 1 cup witch hazel

  • 1 cup water

  • A drop of gentle dish soap

Spray onto a microfiber cloth and wipe each slat. For deeper grime, mist directly onto the blinds and follow with a dry cloth.

2. Shine Chrome and Glass Fixtures

Skip the streaks! Witch hazel dries fast, making it perfect for polishing bathroom faucets, mirrors, and glass surfaces. Spray undiluted witch hazel and wipe clean with a lint-free cloth.

3. Mop High-Shine Floors

Vinyl, tile, and linoleum love this natural shine booster:

  • ½ cup witch hazel

  • 1 gallon warm water

  • Optional: 1 tablespoon borax for tough grime

It lifts residue without leaving dull streaks. Perfect for regular upkeep in busy Saint Louis kitchens and bathrooms.

4. Deodorize Yoga Mats and Home Gym Gear

Mix equal parts witch hazel and distilled water in a spray bottle, then add 10 drops of tea tree oil. After your workout, spritz your mat or gear and let it air dry. It’s gentle on skin, powerful on funk!

5. Gently Clean Jewelry

Use a cotton pad dampened with witch hazel to remove skin oils and debris from rings, earrings, and chains. For a deeper clean, soak pieces for 20–30 minutes, rinse, and dry with a soft cloth.

6. DIY Room Spray That Actually Works

Create a signature scent for your home:

  • 1 part witch hazel

  • 1 part distilled water

  • 10–30 drops of essential oils (lavender, eucalyptus, citrus)

Witch hazel helps oils blend and boosts their staying power. Spritz around rooms, linens, or closets for a quick refresh.

7. Lift Set-In Blood Stains from Fabric

This one’s surprising but effective:

  • Soak the stained fabric area in witch hazel for 30 minutes

  • Rinse with cold water

  • Wash as usual

It helps break down proteins in dried blood, saving your favorite shirt or pillowcase.

8. Remove Sticky Residue

Stubborn sticker goo on glassware or price tag residue? Soak a cotton pad in witch hazel, press it onto the spot for a few minutes, then wipe or gently scrape with a plastic card.

Be Swept Away with Maid 4 Cleaning

Witch hazel is just one of the many tricks we keep up our sleeves at Maid 4 Cleaning. For deep cleaning, move-in/out services, and recurring maintenance, let our trusted team in Saint Louis handle it for you.

📞 Call us today at (314) 626-5885 or visit our website to book your service and be swept away!

items.title: Expert Tips for Removing Oil Stains from Walls
items.summary: If you've encountered oil stains on your walls, you know how they can detract from the aesthetics of your home, no matter the color of the paint or wallpaper. Fortunately, our experts at Maid 4 Cleaning have devised effective and natural methods to tackle those…
items.content_html:
If you've encountered oil stains on your walls, you know how they can detract from the aesthetics of your home, no matter the color of the paint or wallpaper. Fortunately, our experts at Maid 4 Cleaning have devised effective and natural methods to tackle those stubborn oil stains on any surface, from ceramic and wood paneling to drywall. Read on to discover practical ways to clean your walls and maintain their pristine appearance.

Identifying the Origins of Oil Stains on Walls

Those baffling oil spots that suddenly mar your walls can be alarming, but don't stress! Identifying their source is often straightforward.

Start by inspecting nearby appliances or machinery that might be leaking oil, such as stoves or small engines. If no apparent source is found, consider the possibility of deteriorating, oil-based paints. Understanding the origin of these stains is the first step toward effective removal.

Natural Remedies for Oil Spot Removal

Addressing oil stains doesn't require harsh chemicals. Here are some eco-friendly solutions that are just as effective:

  • Baking Soda: This common household item is excellent for absorbing oil. Apply baking soda directly to the stained area and allow it to sit for several hours. Then, wipe away with a damp cloth, gently scrubbing if necessary.

  • Dish Soap: Apply dish soap directly to the oil stain or create a mixture of dish soap and warm water to scrub the area gently. This method is particularly effective for breaking down grease.

  • Vinegar Solution: Vinegar is known for its grease-cutting properties. Mix equal parts of vinegar and water, apply it to the stain with a sponge or cloth, and rinse well to avoid any residual smell.

These natural approaches can help you maintain your walls in top condition without the use of harsh chemicals.

Consider Professional Cleaning Services

For those who find regular home maintenance overwhelming due to busy schedules, Maid 4 Cleaning is here to help. Our professional cleaning services ensure that every corner of your home, including those tricky oil stains, is impeccably clean.

Investing in regular professional cleaning can save you time and stress, allowing you to enjoy a cleaner, healthier living environment. Contact us today for a free estimate and see how easy maintaining a clean home can be with the right help.

Keep Your Walls Spotless with Maid 4 Cleaning

Dealing with food splatters and oil spots is a challenge, especially for cooking enthusiasts or homes with active kids and pets. By identifying the sources of stains and employing natural cleaning solutions, you can keep your walls spotless. At Maid 4 Cleaning, we use eco-friendly products that are tough on stains but gentle on surfaces, ensuring your walls look fresh and clean without risking damage.

Reach out to Maid 4 Cleaning at (314) 626-5885 and let us help you maintain a clean and inviting home environment.

items.title: Optimizing Your Laundry: Should You Wash Sheets and Towels Together?
items.summary: Navigating the dos and don'ts of laundry can be perplexing, especially for new homeowners or those seeking to streamline their household chores. A common query is whether it’s advisable to wash sheets and towels together. Let’s dive into the details to enhance your laundry routine…
items.content_html:
Navigating the dos and don'ts of laundry can be perplexing, especially for new homeowners or those seeking to streamline their household chores. A common query is whether it’s advisable to wash sheets and towels together. Let’s dive into the details to enhance your laundry routine efficiently.

Understanding the Risks of Combining Sheets and Towels in One Load

Mixing sheets and towels in a single laundry load might seem like a time-saver, but it comes with risks. Towels, with their thicker and more absorbent nature, often require more drying time compared to sheets. They can also cause physical damage; towels might snag on sheets during the wash cycle, leading to tears and reducing the lifespan of your delicate linens.

Furthermore, towels tend to shed lint, which can cling to sheets, making them feel rougher. There’s also the risk of cross-contamination; dirt and bacteria from heavily used towels can transfer to sheets, potentially affecting your bedtime comfort.

How to Minimize Damage When Washing Sheets and Towels Together

If you choose to wash sheets and towels together, taking certain precautions can help minimize damage and ensure cleanliness:

  • Avoid Overloading: Ensure your washing machine isn’t overloaded. This gives both sheets and towels enough space to move freely, reducing the risk of snagging and allowing for thorough cleaning.
  • Sort by Fabric Type: Separate heavy towels from lighter sheets. Consider washing heavily soiled towels separately to prevent dirt from transferring.
  • Use Mesh Bags: For extra protection, place delicate sheets in mesh laundry bags. This helps prevent snagging and tangling during the wash cycle.
  • Select the Right Detergent and Settings: Use a high-quality detergent and choose the appropriate wash cycle for the fabric types to ensure effective cleaning without causing damage.

Best Practices for Laundering Towels and Sheets

For the best results, here are some tailored tips:

  • First Wash Separately: Initially wash new towels and sheets separately. This first wash helps set the colors and texture, reducing subsequent lint transfer.
  • Check for Residue: After the first wash, inspect your linens for any residue or leftover dirt. If clean, it may be safe to combine them in future washes, provided the appropriate precautions are taken.
  • Regular Maintenance: Regularly maintain your washer and dryer. A well-maintained machine performs better and reduces the risk of unexpected damages during laundry cycles.

Conclusion: To Combine or Not?

While combining sheets and towels in one wash can save time, doing so requires careful consideration of the types of linens and the conditions of your laundry appliances. For those who prefer not to take any risks, separating towels and sheets remains the safest practice to prolong the life of your linens.

For additional tips and expert cleaning advice, don’t hesitate to explore our blog or contact Maid 4 Cleaning for a free estimate on our home cleaning services. Optimize your home management effectively with our professional insights!
